content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN.

You can perform the following operation on SSS:


Select an index i (1≤i<∣S∣)i \ (1 \leq i \lt |S|)i (1≤i<∣S∣) such that Si=Si+1S_i=S_{i+1}Si​=Si+1​.
Set SiS_iSi​ to 000 and remove Si+1S_{i+1}Si+1​ from the string.
Note that after each operation, the length of SSS decreases by 111.

Is it possible to reduce SSS to a single character by performing the mentioned operation **exactly** N−1N-1N−1 times?


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of SSS.
The second line contains a binary string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, print the answer on a new line: `YES` if it is possible to perform the operation N−1N-1N−1 times, and `NO` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, `YES`, `yes`, `yEs` and `yES` will all be treated as identical.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Perform the operation choosing i=1i = 1i=1. This sets S1=0S_1 = 0S1​=0 and deletes S2S_2S2​ from the string, after which SSS is now a single character.
Test case 222: It is not possible to perform any operation.
Test case 333: One sequence of operations is as follows:

Perform the operation on i=3i = 3i=3. Now, S=110S = 110S=110.
Perform the operation on i=1i = 1i=1. Now, S=00S = 00S=00.
Perform the operation on i=1i = 1i=1. Now, S=0S = 0S=0 and we are done.

Test case 444: It is not possible to perform any operation.
